rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No stale markers, no fixed retrospectives
Register match — Bootstrap voice trio (Godin × Sutherland × Siilasmaa)
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 82: peer-warm closure with earned doubt; line 86 forward-optimist cue; prose reads conversational and direct throughout

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 5: 'strategy framework' used in body without earning it in prior modules; context is that the student has just completed Module 3 exercises, but 'Rumelt's strategy kernel' appears first time mid-exercise in fenced prompt without one-breath primer in preceding body prose
rules_evaluated.fix: Line 13 (body prose before first fence) — add one-breath earning: 'applies a framework like Rumelt's strategy kernel (diagnosis + guiding policy + coherent actions)' OR move the earned landing into the sentence at line 13 before referencing it by name in the prompt

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Earn every technical term on first use
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 13: 'Rumelt's strategy kernel' named without a one-breath primer. The term appears for the first time here and continues into the fence without body-context earning
rules_evaluated.fix: Predecessor sentence (line 13) should anchor it: 'applies a strategy framework — Rumelt's three-part kernel of diagnosis, guiding policy, and coherent actions — inline.' Then the prompt can reference it by name cleanly

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 29: '**Prompt** *(Claude Code)*' has no semantic action lead-in. Prose at lines 14-13 describes the overall move, but no single sentence immediately precedes the fence summarizing 'Ask Claude to spawn three subagents with different stances and synthesize their analysis.'
rules_evaluated.fix: Add before line 29: 'Ask Claude to spawn three subagents, each with a different strategic stance, and synthesize their notes into a coherent answer.' (or similar one-liner under 20 words)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact destinations are explicit subpaths, never naked filenames (Bootstrap-specific)
rules_evaluated.evidence: line 32 / 51: 'writes a short note (under 200 words) to module-3/stances/' is explicit subpath, good
lines 82-86 hold the deliberate unease (doubt-naming, 'can't yet say which is which, that feeling is correct') and frame the stew for Module 5 hand-off. Mood is honored.

dropped.rule_lead: Tell me what you wrote, not show me what you'll write (post-action over pre-confirm for non-irreversible actions)
dropped.evidence: line 43 (Code fence) and line 62 (Cowork fence): 'Show me before saving.' This is appending text to an existing file in the student's session — non-irreversible (can be re-run, can be overwritten). Pre-confirm is overkill.
